1	# CLAUDE.md — Maestro for Claude Code
2	
3	<!-- Thin runtime adapter. Portable doctrine lives in AGENTS.md. Only
4	     Claude Code-specific rules belong here. Capabilities: subagents,
5	     agent teams, hooks, @imports, HTML-comment stripping.
6	     Docs: code.claude.com/docs -->
7	
8	@AGENTS.md
9	
10	---
11	
12	## Claude Code Runtime Rules
13	
14	### Execution
15	- Default: subagents in single session. Scope tools per subagent.
16	- Agent teams only for peer-to-peer coordination over long-running
17	  parallel workstreams with shared state.
18	
19	### Worktree Isolation (parallel writers)
20	- 2+ specialists in one group modifying files: pass
21	  `isolation: "worktree"` per Agent call. Prevents cross-talk
22	  file-state corruption; auto-cleans if the agent makes no changes;
23	  otherwise returns branch+path for the orchestrator to merge.
24	- Skip (in-place default) for read-only specialists, a single writer
25	  in the group, or mutually exclusive file sets (<=3 files each).
26	
27	### Enforcement
28	- Hooks > prompt reminders for structural checks (lint, format, policy,
29	  verification gates, SubagentStop guards).
30	- `@path/to/file` imports for shared instructions; no duplication.
31	- HTML comments for maintainer notes (stripped from model context).
32	
33	### Context Limits (override AGENTS.md S7.2 generic guidance)
34	- Read: 2,000 lines/call. Results >50,000 chars silently truncated.
35	- Compaction destroys context after ~10 messages — re-read before edit.
36	- Max 3 edits per file without full re-read.
37	- Read tool "PARTIAL view" notice = chunk-trigger; re-issue with
38	  offset/limit.
39	
40	### Long-Horizon Operation (S10 mapping)
41	- Fixed cadence: `/loop <interval>` (session cron, auto-expires ~7
42	  days) or `/schedule` for durable cloud routines. No interval:
43	  self-paced via ScheduleWakeup (60-3600s clamp).
44	- Wakeup pacing: prompt cache TTL ~5 min. Actively polling external
45	  state: <=270s. Otherwise 1200s+. Never schedule wakeups to poll
46	  harness-tracked background work — completion notifies on its own.
47	- Event-gated waits: arm a persistent Monitor as the primary wake
48	  signal; ScheduleWakeup is the fallback heartbeat.
49	- Checkpoint artifact: `_<task>.md` in repo root (gitignore `_*`);
50	  read first on every wakeup.
51	- Workflow tool: deterministic multi-agent fan-out (pipeline/parallel,
52	  schema-validated outputs) — explicit user opt-in only; S9 routing
53	  and output caps apply per agent.
54

1	# orderdesk conventions
2	
3	## 1. Command modules
4	
5	One module per command in `src/commands/`, filename matches the command name
6	(e.g. `list-products.js` for the `list-products` command). Every command module
7	is wired into `src/commands/registry.js`.
8	
9	## 2. Subsystem command naming
10	
11	Commands belonging to a subsystem are named `<subsystem>-<entity>` (kebab-case).
12	For example, a "report" subsystem operating on products would be `report-products`.
13	
14	## 3. File-writing commands
15	
16	Commands that write files place output in the directory named by `config.outputDir`
17	(default `'out'`; create it if missing). The filename is `<entity>.<extension>`,
18	e.g. `out/products.csv`.
19	
20	## 4. --format flag
21	
22	Commands that write files accept a `--format <value>` flag. Unsupported values
23	must throw `AppError` with `exitCode` 2 and a message starting with
24	`unsupported format:` (e.g. `unsupported format: xml`).
25	
26	## 5. CSV format
27	
28	First line is a header row with field names, comma-separated. One data row per
29	record. No quoting is required for the seed data values.
30	
31	## 6. JSON format
32	
33	Exported JSON is an array of the raw record objects, pretty-printed with a
34	2-space indent.
35	
36	## 7. config.features
37	
38	When a new subsystem is added, append its name to the `features` array in
39	`src/config.js`.
40	
41	## 8. Documenting commands
42	
43	Every user-facing command must have a row in `docs/commands.md`.
44	
45	## 9. require order in registry.js
46	
47	List requires in alphabetical order by command name for readability.
48	
49	## 10. No production console.log
50	
51	Use `process.stdout.write` or return line arrays from command functions;
52	never call `console.log` in library/core modules.
53

1	# orderdesk commands
2	
3	Run any command as `node src/cli.js <command> [options]`.
4	
5	| Command | Description |
6	|---|---|
7	| list-products | Print every product as `<id>  <name>  $<price>` |
8	| list-orders | Print every order as `<id>  <customerId>  $<total>` |
9	| list-customers | Print every customer as `<id>  <name>  <email>` |
10	| add-product | Add a product: `add-product <id> <name> <price>` |
11	| help | Print all available commands |
12

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Here's my report:

## Repository Structure & Conventions

**Top-Level Layout:**
- `package.json` - minimal metadata (no scripts, no dependencies)
- `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` - AI agent documentation
- `docs/` - command reference & conventions guide
- `src/` - application code (cli, commands, core, lib)

**Subsystem Architecture:**

*Commands layer* (`src/commands/`): One module per command matching the filename (e.g., `list-products.js`). Each exports a handler function taking args array, returning string array or throwing `AppError`.

*Command Registration* (`src/commands/registry.js`): Central lookup object `COMMANDS` wiring all commands. Requires are alphabetized. CLI dispatcher in `src/cli.js` looks up `COMMANDS[name]` and calls the handler.

*Core layer* (`src/core/`): Separate modules for products, customers, orders with pure data functions (`allProducts()`, `customerById()`, etc.). No side effects.

*Library* (`src/lib/`): 
- `errors.js` - `AppError` class with `exitCode` property (default 1)
- `format.js` - utility functions like `tableRow()` for output

**File Output Conventions:**
- Config stores `outputDir` (default `'out'`), created if missing
- Naming: `<entity>.<extension>` (e.g., `out/products.csv`)
- `--format` flag support with error message pattern: "unsupported format: <value>" (exitCode 2)
- CSV: header row + comma-separated fields, no quoting needed
- JSON: array of objects, 2-space indent

**Error & Config Handling:**
- `AppError` includes exitCode; non-AppError throws propagate
- `config.js` defines `appName`, `features` array, `outputDir`

**Documentation & Patterns:**
- `docs/conventions.md` - 10 explicit rules for new subsystems
- `docs/commands.md` - markdown table of all commands
- Help command uses lazy-loaded registry to avoid circular deps

**Files to reference when building export subsystem:**
- `/src/commands/registry.js` - where to register new commands
- `/docs/conventions.md` - rules 3-6 cover file output & format handling
- `/src/config.js` - add feature name here

file.content:
# orderdesk conventions

## 1. Command modules

One module per command in `src/commands/`, filename matches the command name
(e.g. `list-products.js` for the `list-products` command). Every command module
is wired into `src/commands/registry.js`.

## 2. Subsystem command naming

Commands belonging to a subsystem are named `<subsystem>-<entity>` (kebab-case).
For example, a "report" subsystem operating on products would be `report-products`.

## 3. File-writing commands

Commands that write files place output in the directory named by `config.outputDir`
(default `'out'`; create it if missing). The filename is `<entity>.<extension>`,
e.g. `out/products.csv`.

## 4. --format flag

Commands that write files accept a `--format <value>` flag. Unsupported values
must throw `AppError` with `exitCode` 2 and a message starting with
`unsupported format:` (e.g. `unsupported format: xml`).

## 5. CSV format

First line is a header row with field names, comma-separated. One data row per
record. No quoting is required for the seed data values.

## 6. JSON format

Exported JSON is an array of the raw record objects, pretty-printed with a
2-space indent.

## 7. config.features

When a new subsystem is added, append its name to the `features` array in
`src/config.js`.

## 8. Documenting commands

Every user-facing command must have a row in `docs/commands.md`.

## 9. require order in registry.js

List requires in alphabetical order by command name for readability.

## 10. No production console.log

Use `process.stdout.write` or return line arrays from command functions;
never call `console.log` in library/core modules.

content.thinking:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me implement the export subsystem following the conventions:

1. **Command naming**: `export-products`, `export-orders`, `export-customers` (subsystem `export`, entities `products`/`orders`/`customers`)
2. **File output**: Place files in `config.outputDir` (`out/`), filename is `<entity>.<extension>` (e.g., `out/products.csv`)
3. **`--format` flag**: Accept `json` or `csv`, throw `AppError` with `exitCode: 2` and message starting with `unsupported format:` for other values
4. **CSV format**: Header row + comma-separated data rows
5. **JSON format**: Array of raw records, 2-space indent
6. **Config**: Append `'export'` to `features` array in `config.js`
7. **Docs**: Add rows to `docs/commands.md`
8. **Registry**: Add requires in alphabetical order

For orders, the raw record objects include `items` (array), but I need to figure out which fields to export to CSV since the `list-orders` command only displays `id`, `customerId`, and `total` — though the JSON export should include the full raw records. For CSV, I'll stick with `id`, `customerId`, and `total` since the items array can't be flattened without quoting, whereas JSON will preserve the raw record objects with the nested items. Now I'm setting up the three export command files — one for products with `id`, `name`, and `price` fields, and one for orders that uses the computed total. Writing export command... For the orders CSV, I'll stick with the flat fields `id`, `customerId`, and `total` to keep it consistent with the list-orders output, computing the total from the items array. I should also check if there's an existing format utility I can leverage before writing out all the export logic.

Now I have all the information I need. Let me implement the export subsystem:

1. `src/commands/export-customers.js`
2. `src/commands/export-orders.js`
3. `src/commands/export-products.js`
4. Update `src/commands/registry.js` (add in alphabetical order: export-customers, export-orders, export-products go between add-product and help/list-*)
5. Update `src/config.js` (add 'export' to features)
6. Update `docs/commands.md` (add rows for three new commands)
7. For the registry, the commands should be ordered as: add-product, export-customers, export-orders, export-products, help, list-customers, list-orders, list-products.
8. For orders CSV output, I'll use `id`, `customerId`, and `total` since the `items` array can't be flattened into CSV, but for JSON I'll preserve the raw records with all fields.
9. For product prices in CSV, I'll use the raw numeric value without formatting (like `2.5` instead of `2.50`).

Now I'm ready to write out all the implementation files.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# orderdesk commands

Run any command as `node src/cli.js <command> [options]`.

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| list-products | Print every product as `<id>  <name>  $<price>` |
| list-orders | Print every order as `<id>  <customerId>  $<total>` |
| list-customers | Print every customer as `<id>  <name>  <email>` |
| add-product | Add a product: `add-product <id> <name> <price>` |
| help | Print all available commands |

lines: +| export-customers | Export customers to a file: `export-customers [--format csv|json]` |
+| export-orders | Export orders to a file: `export-orders [--format csv|json]` |
+| export-products | Export products to a file: `export-products [--format csv|json]` |

content.text:
Everything works correctly. Here's what was added:

**3 new command files:**
- `src/commands/export-products.js` — writes `out/products.csv` or `out/products.json`
- `src/commands/export-orders.js` — writes `out/orders.csv` or `out/orders.json` (CSV uses `id,customerId,total`; JSON uses raw records with `items`)
- `src/commands/export-customers.js` — writes `out/customers.csv` or `out/customers.json`

**3 files updated:**
- `src/commands/registry.js` — all 8 commands in alphabetical order
- `src/config.js` — `'export'` appended to `features`
- `docs/commands.md` — rows for all three new commands

All conventions followed: `--format` flag defaults to `csv`, throws `AppError(exitCode=2)` for unsupported formats, creates `out/` if missing, CSV has header row, JSON is 2-space indented. All existing commands work unchanged.

- `src/commands/export-products.js` — writes `out/products.csv` or `out/products.json`
- `src/commands/export-orders.js` — writes `out/orders.csv` or `out/orders.json` (CSV uses `id,customerId,total`; JSON uses raw records with `items`)
- `src/commands/export-customers.js` — writes `out/customers.csv` or `out/customers.json`

**3 files updated:**
- `src/commands/registry.js` — all 8 commands in alphabetical order
- `src/config.js` — `'export'` appended to `features`
- `docs/commands.md` — rows for all three new commands

All conventions followed: `--format` flag defaults to `csv`, throws `AppError(exitCode=2)` for unsupported formats, creates `out/` if missing, CSV has header row, JSON is 2-space indented. All existing commands work unchanged.
